channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets

item.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
item.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity....

item.title: Chinese hackers use new Atlas RAT malware in European cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese-speaking cybercrime group has expanded its targeting to the European space, deploying previously undocumented malware and the Atlas backdoor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
item.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: U.S. sanctions Nobitex crypto exchange used by Iranian ransomware actors
item.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has announced sanctions against Nobitex, Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, for facilitating payments related to terrorist activities. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
item.description: CISA, the FBI, the NSA, the Department of Energy, and other US government partners are warning that hackers are targeting internet-exposed automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems used to monitor fuel and l...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
item.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
item.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
item.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New 'HTTP/2 Bomb' DoS attack crashes web servers in under a minute
item.description: A new denial-of-service (DoS) attack dubbed HTTP/2 Bomb can be launched from a single machine to take down web servers within seconds. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
item.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google DoubleClick Abused in New Malspam Campaign to Deliver DesckVB RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malspam campaign that makes use of Google's DoubleClick domain as a way to evade detection and ultimately deliver a remote access trojan (RAT) named DesckV...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA warns of active attacks exploiting Android, Linux bugs
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel and Android operating system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Beyond the Zero-Day: See Your Network Like an Attacker | Webinar with HD Moore
item.description: Assume the breach. Zero-days keep shipping, AI is writing exploits faster than anyone patches, and "patch everything in time" stopped working years ago. Stop betting the org on winning that race. You ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
item.description:
A development flag left switched on in production builds of several Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled the check that limits account-token sharing to trusted Microsoft apps.
Any other app on the sam...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: What 345 Days of Untested Exposure Looks Like at a Bank
item.description: A two-week penetration test can leave roughly 345 days of real-world exposure unvalidated. Sprocket Security explores why continuous testing is becoming critical as attack surfaces constantly change. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
item.description: Redis has patched a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. The flaw was found by an autonomous AI t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token.
"Just by clicking a link, it's possible f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
item.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.description:
The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity
Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of applications, d...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Acer working to patch max severity zero-days in Wave 7 routers
item.description: Acer is working to address two maximum-severity zero-day vulnerabilities affecting its Wave 7 mesh routers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an unpatched issue that could be exploited to disclose a user's NTLMv2 hash to the attacker.
Like in the case of CVE-2026-33829, which impacted the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Police dismantles 9 crime groups in illegal streaming crackdown
item.description: European and international law enforcement agencies have dismantled nine organized crime groups and arrested 29 suspects in a major crackdown on illegal streaming operations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
item.description: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google adds Android protection against AI deepfake scam calls
item.description: Google is introducing a new Android security feature that will detect and flag phone calls in which scammers use artificial intelligence to impersonate a user's personal contacts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New HTTP/2 Bomb Vulnerability Allows Remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote denial-of-service exploit that affects major web servers, including NGINX, Apache HTTPD, Microsoft IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare Pingora.
The vulnerabi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: VS Code zero-day lets hackers steal GitHub tokens in one click
item.description: A security researcher has released exploit code for a Visual Studio Code (VS Code) zero-day vulnerability that allows attackers to steal GitHub authentication tokens by tricking users into clicking a ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Weedhack Attacks Minecraft Users, CountLoader Hits 86K, Miners Spread via Pirated Content
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new campaign targeting Minecraft players via YouTube to spread malware capable of gaining control of victims' systems.
The Minecraft-focused malware-as-a-serv...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
